ddls:
CREATE TABLE "models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of the model (e.g., classification, regression) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the model was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the model was last updated */,
  "trained_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who trained the model */,
  "hyperparameters" TEXT /* Model hyperparameters in JSON format */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "training_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each training session */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being trained */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the training session */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the training session */,
  "learning_rate" REAL /* Learning rate used during training */,
  "training_loss" REAL /* Training loss achieved during training */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the training session */,
  "trained_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who initiated the training session */,
  "training_config" TEXT /* Training configuration in JSON format */,
  "gpu_usage" REAL /* GPU usage during the training session */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage during the training session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_training_sessions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "evaluation_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each evaluation metric */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being evaluated */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the evaluation metric */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the evaluation metric */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of evaluation metric */,
  "evaluation_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the evaluation metric was calculated */,
  "model_version" INTEGER /* Version of the model being evaluated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_evaluation_metrics_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bad_epochs" (
  "bad_epoch_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bad epoch */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the training session */,
  "epoch_number" INTEGER /* Epoch number that was bad */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the bad epoch */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message associated with the bad epoch */,
  "solution" TEXT /* Solution implemented to resolve the bad epoch */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bad_epoch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bad_epochs_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES training_sessions ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model version */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model version */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the model version was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the model version was last updated */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the changes made in the model version */,
  "parent_version" INTEGER /* Version ID of the parent model version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_versions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time of access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access */,
  "accessed_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the model was accessed */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed on the model */,
  "accessed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)
